title: 2020 AMC 10A Problems/Problem 4
problem: A driver travels for $2$ hours at $60$ miles per hour, during which her car gets $30$ miles per gallon of gasoline. She is paid $\$0.50$ per mile, and her only expense is gasoline at $\$2.00$ per gallon. What is her net rate of pay, in dollars per hour, after this expense? $\textbf{(A)}\ 20\qquad\textbf{(B)}\ 22\qquad\textbf{(C)}\ 24\qquad\textbf{(D)}\ 25\qquad\textbf{(E)}\ 26$
solution:
Since the driver travels $60$ miles per hour and each hour she uses $2$ gallons of gasoline, she spends $\$4$ per hour on gas. If she gets $\$0.50$ per mile, then she gets $\$30$ per hour of driving. Subtracting the gas cost, her net rate of money earned per hour is $\boxed{\textbf{(E)}\ 26}$ .
~mathsmiley
